title: Analyze and record first 3 candidate sources (at least 1 approved)
context: Before any audio or metadata is ingested the license reviewer must analyse candidate sources and populate sources/allowlist.yml. The first three candidates expected are Wikidata (CC0 - likely auto-approved), GeoNames (CC-BY - allowed with attribution), and Wikimedia Commons/Lingua Libre (per-file CC - needs per-file verification process documented). Each entry must be verified against its actual current license terms; PD originals and PD scans/recordings must be distinguished. At least one source must achieve 'approved' status before M0 can exit. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-license-001 for the allow-list schema.
objective: Research and document at least 3 candidate sources in sources/allowlist.yml using the ratified schema: verify each source's current license terms, record the attribution string, determine approved/rejected/pending status, and note any per-file verification requirements or share-alike implications. At least 1 source must reach 'approved' status.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 candidate sources are documented in sources/allowlist.yml with all required fields populated (title, custodian, url, format, licenseBasis, attributionString, status, notes).
Each entry's license is verified against the source's current terms at the time of analysis, not assumed from memory.
PD originals are distinguished from PD scans/recordings where relevant (e.g., a historical text vs a modern recording of it).
At least 1 source achieves status: approved, with a complete attribution string.
Wikidata (CC0), GeoNames (CC-BY), and Wikimedia Commons/Lingua Libre (per-file CC) are among the sources analysed.
Any source with per-file license variability documents the per-file verification process required before use.
License reviewer signs off all entries before the task is marked done.
output: Updated sources/allowlist.yml with at least 3 candidate sources analysed and at least 1 approved, committed via PR with license reviewer sign-off.
